briefdescription.para: Use antenna identifier 1 for CS_SYNC packets.
Use antenna identifier 2 for CS_SYNC packets.
Use antenna identifier 3 for CS_SYNC packets.
Use antenna identifier 4 for CS_SYNC packets.
Use antennas in repetitive order from 1 to 4 for CS_SYNC packets.
No recommendation for local controller antenna selection.
CS Test CS_SYNC Antenna Identifier.
CS SNR control options.
Initiator and reflector tones sent without tone extension.
Initiator tone sent with extension, reflector tone sent without tone extension.
Initiator tone sent without extension, reflector tone sent with tone extension.
Initiator and reflector tones sent with tone extension.
Applicable for mode-2 and mode-3 only:
CS Test Override 3 T_PM Tone Extension.
Loop through all valid Antenna Permutation Indices starting from the lowest index.
CS Test Override 4 Tone Antenna Permutation.

detaileddescription.para: These values represent indices in an antenna path permutation table.
Which table is applicable (and which indices are valid) depends on the maximum number of antenna paths (N_AP).
If N_AP = 2, the permutation table is:
If N_AP = 3, the permutation table is:
If N_AP = 4, the permutation table is:

briefdescription.para: Loop through pattern '0011' and '1100' (in transmission order)
CS Test Override 7 Sounding Sequence Marker Value.
PRBS9 payload sequence.
Repeated '11110000' payload sequence.
Repeated '10101010' payload sequence.
PRBS15 payload sequence.
Repeated '11111111' payload sequence.
Repeated '00000000' payload sequence.
Repeated '00001111' payload sequence.
Repeated '01010101' payload sequence.
Custom payload provided by the user.
CS Test Override 8 CS_SYNC Payload Pattern.
Write CS configuration in local Controller only
Write CS configuration in both local and remote Controller using Channel Sounding Configuration procedure.
CS config creation context.
Extract in-phase and quadrature terms from HCI-formatted PCT.

detaileddescription.para: Convenience function for processing 24-bit phase correction terms found in CS step data. The 12-bit signed real and imaginary components are converted to host endianness and sign-extended.

simplesect.para: struct bt_le_cs_iq_sample containing real and imaginary terms as int16_t

briefdescription.para: Set all valid channel map bits.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to enable all valid channels in a given CS channel map

briefdescription.para: Read Remote Supported Capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to query the CS capabilities that are supported by the remote controller.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Set Channel Sounding default settings.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to set default Channel Sounding settings for this connection.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Read Remote FAE Table.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to read the per-channel mode-0 Frequency Actuation Error table of the remote Controller.

If the remote controller supports a Frequency Actuation Error of zero relative to its mode-0 transmissions in the reflector role (see bt_conn_le_cs_capabilities::cs_without_fae_supported), calling this function is not needed and the read remote FAE table complete event will be generated with an error code.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Register callbacks for the CS Test mode.

detaileddescription.para: Existing callbacks can be unregistered by providing NULL function pointers.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING_TEST must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Set of callbacks to be used with CS Test

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Start a CS test.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to start a CS test where the IUT is placed in the role of either the initiator or reflector.

The first mode-0 channel in the list is used as the starting channel for the test. At the beginning of any test, the IUT in the reflector role shall listen on the first mode-0 channel until it receives the first transmission from the initiator. Similarly, with the IUT in the initiator role, the tester will start by listening on the first mode-0 channel and the IUT shall transmit on that channel for the first half of the first CS step. Thereafter, the parameters of this command describe the required transmit and receive behavior for the CS test.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING_TEST must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_conn *conn, struct bt_le_cs_create_config_params *params, enum bt_le_cs_create_config_context context)
memberdef.name: bt_le_cs_create_config

briefdescription.para: Create CS configuration.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to create a new CS configuration or update an existing one with the config id specified.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Controls whether the configuration is written to the local controller or both the local and the remote controller

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Remove CS configuration.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to remove a CS configuration from the local controller identified by the config_id

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Stop ongoing CS Test.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to stop any CS test that is in progress.
The controller is expected to finish reporting any subevent results before completing this termination.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING_TEST must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Parse CS Subevent Step Data.

detaileddescription.para: A helper for parsing HCI-formatted step data found in channel sounding subevent results.
A typical use-case is filtering out data which does not meet certain packet quality or NADM requirements.

simplesect.para: This function will consume the data when parsing.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a buffer containing the step data.
Callback function which will be called for each step data found. The callback should return true to continue parsing, or false to stop.
User data to be passed to the callback.

briefdescription.para: CS Security Enable.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to start or restart the Channel Sounding Security Start procedure in the local Controller for the ACL connection identified in the conn parameter.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Procedure Enable.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to enable or disable the scheduling of CS procedures by the local Controller, with the remote device identified in the conn parameter.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Parameters for the CS Procedure Enable command.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Set Procedure Parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to set the parameters for the scheduling of one or more CS procedures by the local controller.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Parameters for the CS Set Procedure Parameters command.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Set Channel Classification.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to update the channel classification based on its local information.
The nth bitfield (in the range 0 to 78) contains the value for the CS channel index n. Channel Enabled = 1; Channel Disabled = 0.
Channels n = 0, 1, 23, 24, 25, 77, and 78 shall be reserved for future use and shall be set to zero. At least 15 channels shall be enabled.
The most significant bit (bit 79) is reserved for future use.

simplesect.para: To use this API, CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.
Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Read Local Supported Capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to read the CS capabilities that are supported by the local Controller.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Return values for the CS Procedure Enable command.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Read Local Supported Capabilities V2.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to read the CS capabilities that are supported by the local Controller.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Return values for the CS Procedure Enable command.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Write Cached Remote Supported Capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to write the cached copy of the CS capabilities that are supported by the remote Controller for the connection identified.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Parameters for the CS Write Cached Remote Supported Capabilities command.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Write Cached Remote Supported Capabilities V2.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to write the cached copy of the CS capabilities that are supported by the remote Controller for the connection identified.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Parameters for the CS Write Cached Remote Supported Capabilities command.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: CS Write Cached Remote FAE Table.

detaileddescription.para: This command is used to write a cached copy of the per-channel mode-0 Frequency Actuation Error table of the remote device in the local Controller.

If the remote controller supports a Frequency Actuation Error of zero relative to its mode-0 transmissions in the reflector role (see bt_conn_le_cs_capabilities::cs_without_fae_supported), calling this function is not needed. An error code will be returned.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: Per-channel mode-0 FAE table of the local Controller

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get antenna path used for the CS tone exchange when using multiple antenna paths for mode-2 or mode-3 CS procedure.

detaileddescription.para: The function implements antenna path permutation defined in Bluetooth Core Specification 6.0, Vol. 6, Part H, Section 4.7.5.

simplesect.para: To use this API CONFIG_BT_CHANNEL_SOUNDING must be set.

parameterdescription.para: The number of antenna paths, range: [1, 4].
Index of the tone in the CS step, range [0, n_ap]. tone_index = n_ap corresponds to extension slot.

simplesect.para: Antenna path used to exchange CS tones, range: [0, 3].
-EINVAL if arguments are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Macro for getting a specific channel bit in CS channel map.

simplesect.para: Bit value, either 1 or 0

briefdescription.para: Macro for setting a specific channel bit value in CS channel map.

parameterdescription.para: Bit value to be set, either 1 or 0

briefdescription.para: LE Channel Sounding (CS)
